articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

article.headline: Argentina, without Messi, beat El Salvador in Copa tune-up
article.description: Argentina began preparations for their Copa America defence with a convincing 3-0 win against El Salvador on Friday.
article.linkText: Argentina, without Messi, win in Copa tune-up

images.name: Cristian Romero Argentina goal celeb vs. El Salvador March 2024 [1296x729]

peers.name: Cristian Romero Argentina goal celeb vs. El Salvador March 2024 [1296x1296]
Cristian Romero Argentina goal celeb vs. El Salvador March 2024 [1296x729]
Cristian Romero Argentina goal celeb vs. El Salvador March 2024 [1296x864]
Cristian Romero Argentina goal celeb vs. El Salvador March 2024 [1296x518]

article.story:
Argentina began preparations for their Copa America defence with a convincing 3-0 win against El Salvador on Friday.

The 2021 champions were without captain Lionel Messi due to injury but prevailed at Lincoln Financial Field in Philadelphia thanks to goals from Cristian Romero, Enzo Fernández and Giovani Lo Celso.

- Stream on ESPN+: LaLiga, Bundesliga, more (U.S.)

Messi will also miss the international friendly against Costa Rica in Los Angeles next week after hurting his hamstring during Inter Miami CF's Concacaf Champions Cup win against Nashville earlier this month.

But the eight-time Ballon d'Or winner was not a big miss for world champions Argentina against an El Salvador side that has now gone 21 games without a win.

Tottenham Hotspur defender Romero put the Albiceleste ahead on 16 minutes with a header from an Ángel Di María corner kick.

A second goal came before half-time when Chelsea midfielder Fernandez scored from close range in the 42nd minute.

Lo Celso completed the scoring seven minutes into the second half after good work by Di Maria and Lautaro Martínez to set him up.

Youngsters Alejandro Garnacho and Valentín Barco were both given minutes off the bench by Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni after impressing for Manchester United and the Under-23 team in Olympic qualifying respectively.

keyEvents.text: Goal!  Argentina 1, El Salvador 0. Cristian Romero (Argentina) header from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Ángel Di María with a cross   following a corner.
Goal!  Argentina 2, El Salvador 0. Enzo Fernández (Argentina) right footed shot from very close range to the top left corner.
Substitution, Argentina. Alejandro Garnacho replaces Nicolás González.
Substitution, Argentina. Nicolás Otamendi replaces Enzo Fernández.
Narciso Orellana (El Salvador) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  Argentina 3, El Salvador 0. Giovani Lo Celso (Argentina) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Lautaro Martínez.
Substitution, Argentina. Valentín Barco replaces Nicolás Tagliafico.
Jorge Cruz (El Salvador) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Argentina. Facundo Buonanotte replaces Rodrigo De Paul.
Substitution, El Salvador. Julio Sibrián replaces Bryan Tamacas.
Substitution, El Salvador. Pablo Punyed replaces Narciso Orellana.
Substitution, Argentina. Nahuel Molina replaces Cristian Romero.
Substitution, El Salvador. Melvin Cartagena replaces Darwin Cerén.
Substitution, Argentina. Alexis Mac Allister replaces Giovani Lo Celso.
Substitution, El Salvador. Jeremy Garay replaces Nelson Bonilla because of an injury.
Substitution, El Salvador. Diego Flores replaces Nelson Flores.
Substitution, El Salvador. German Fuentes replaces Jairo Henríquez.
Second Half ends, Argentina 3, El Salvador 0.
